macro_segments.title: Introduction to Rabbi Shimon's Position on Husband's Rights to Wife's Property and the Doctrine of Concealment (Shtar Mavrachat)

micro_segments.title: Overview of Rabbi Shimon's Distinction Regarding Known vs. Unknown Property and the Husband's Right to Block Sales
Case Story: Woman Transferring Property to Daughter to Hide from Husband; Rav Nachman Tears Up the Document
Rav Anan Challenges Rav Nachman; Mar Ukva Explains the Concept of Shtar Mavrachat (Document to Chase Away Property)
Shmuel's Authority and His Position on Tearing Up Concealment Documents; Rava Examines the Reasoning
The Principle That One Does Not Abandon Oneself to Give Property to Others; Application to Daughters

macro_segments.title: The Tosefta's Remedies for Women to Protect Property and the Debate Between Shimon ben Menasya and the Sages

micro_segments.title: Tosefta: Woman Writing Fake Documents to Others; Shimon ben Menasya vs. Sages on the Validity of Such Transfers
The Requirement for Explicit Conditional Language (From Today and When I Desire) to Protect the Transfer
Gemara's Question: If the Transfer is Fake, How Does the Wife Keep Property from the Husband?
Abaye's Resolution: The Transfer Creates Property Unknown to the Husband (Nichsaim She-Einam Yeduyim) Per Rabbi Shimon
Rabbeinu Tam's Distinction: Shtar Mavrachat Works Only Where a Legal Category Exists for Unknown Property; Not for Other Creditors

macro_segments.title: The Mishnah on Property Inherited After Betrothal and the Husband's Right to Convert It into Income-Generating Assets

micro_segments.title: Mishnah: Property Falling to the Wife Before Betrothal; When Must Money Be Converted into Land?
Rabbi Meir on Attached Fruit: Valuing the Difference Between Field With and Without Fruit
Mishnah's Distribution of Attached vs. Detached Fruit Between Wife (Attached on Entry/Exit) and Husband (Detached Produce)
Rabbi Shimon's Position: Strength Increases for Wife at Exit Where It Decreased at Entry

macro_segments.title: The Gemara's Analysis of Investment Choices and the Preference for Protecting the Principal

micro_segments.title: Gemara: Choice Between Field and Rental Property; Tosafot's Reading That Wife Can Insist on Principal Protection
Escalating Debates: Field vs. Houses vs. Palm Trees vs. Other Trees; Principle of Secure vs. Risk-bearing Assets
Timber and Fish: Whether Non-Regenerating Resources Count as Principal or Fruit; The Rule on Regeneration (Gazao Machilif)

macro_segments.title: The Case of Offspring of Animals and Slaves in Dowry Property; The Concept of Fruit of Fruit

micro_segments.title: Baraita: Child of Animal Belongs to Husband (Fruit); Child of Slave Belongs to Wife; Disagreement of Chananyah ben Akaviah
Explanation That Husband Gets Fruit But Not Fruit-of-Fruit (Peira de-Peira); The Case of Kefel (Double Payment)
Tosafot's Irony: Theft of Original Animal vs. Theft of Offspring; Husband's Kefel vs. Wife's Kefel
Concern for Principal Deterioration (Death of Animals): Wife Retains Offspring of Slave to Reinvest and Protect Principal
Exception for Animals: Husband Does Not Get Kefel Because Hides Provide Some Minimal Residual Value to Wife

topical_tags.term: Shtar Mavrachat (Document to Hide/Chase Away Property)
Nichsaim She-Einam Yeduyim (Property Unknown to Husband)
Mar Ukva (Explains Doctrine to Rav Anan)
Rabbeinu Tam (Limits Shtar Mavrachat to Wife-Husband Cases)
Vlad Behemah Milog (Offspring of Dowry Animals)
Vlad Shifcha Milog (Offspring of Dowry Slaves)
Chananyah ben Akaviah (Disagrees on Slave Offspring)
